scope_advisory_reference: automation_bus/latest_scope_advisory.md — P2-1 Prose Substrate Cohort (2026-06-26). Advisory recommended STANDARD/CONTENT predating STOP-1 finding. GPT escalation to HIGH/MIXED after STOP-1 is architecturally correct.

changes.evidence: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:2-6 — work_id:P2-1, branch:feature/prose-substrate-wave1-wired, risk_level:HIGH, execution_model:SINGLE_PHASE, change_type:MIXED. All five mandatory fields present.

HIGH justified: narrative_compiler_lc_s3_assembly_v1.py is the output assembly module and narrative_report_compiler_v1.py is the narrative compiler — both qualify as Intelligence Core under SOP §10 ('output assembly'). Any modification to Intelligence Core is HIGH by default. Scope advisory STANDARD/CONTENT classification predated STOP-1 which revealed Python changes to these files are required for runtime-reachable prose. MIXED justified: sprint contains both BEHAVIOUR changes (_LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS additions in two Intelligence Core Python files) and CONTENT changes (KB YAML authoring). MIXED governs under BEHAVIOUR controls per SOP §4. Both classifications confirmed correct post-STOP-1.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md: six sequential phases with no gated allowlist switch separating package validation from engine activation. No TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH trigger: _LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS is a local constant in each Python file, not an assembler-layer allowlist requiring deferred activation. SINGLE_PHASE correct.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:91-96 — 'Only these Python files may be modified: backend/core/analytics/narrative_compiler_lc_s3_assembly_v1.py; backend/core/analytics/narrative_report_compiler_v1.py'. Acceptance criteria AC3: 'Only the two declared Python files are modified for behaviour wiring'. No other Python files appear under behaviour scope.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:99-116 — allowed changes limited to extending _LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS with signal_iron_low, signal_iron_high, signal_free_t3_low, signal_tpo_ab_high. Explicit prohibitions listed: routing logic, entity loop, compiler structure, payload structure, infer_yaml_flags_from_payload() logic, _SECONDARY_SIGNAL_HINTS, scoring/ranking/signal activation/domain assembler logic. Acceptance criteria AC4 confirms. Phase 2 repeats identical constraint.

narrative_compiler_lc_s3_assembly_v1.py:32-37 — _LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS = frozenset({'signal_homocysteine_high', 'signal_homocysteine_elevation_context', 'signal_mcv_high'}). narrative_report_compiler_v1.py:38-42 — _LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS: Set[str] = {'signal_homocysteine_high', 'signal_homocysteine_elevation_context', 'signal_mcv_high'}. Members identical. signal_iron_low, signal_iron_high, signal_free_t3_low, signal_tpo_ab_high absent from both. SOP Phase 1 STOP condition ('STOP if the two Python files use incompatible hint mechanisms') will pass.

narrative_compiler_lc_s3_assembly_v1.py:39-47 — _SECONDARY_SIGNAL_HINTS = frozenset({'signal_ldl_cholesterol_high', 'signal_hdl_cholesterol_high', 'signal_non_hdl_high', 'signal_lipid_transport_dysfunction', 'signal_triglycerides_high'}). narrative_report_compiler_v1.py:43-49 — identical members as Set[str]. Both secondary sets match. SOP prohibition on modifying _SECONDARY_SIGNAL_HINTS correctly scoped.

narrative_compiler_lc_s3_assembly_v1.py:32 uses frozenset({...}); narrative_report_compiler_v1.py:38 uses Set[str] = {...}. Members are currently identical, so this is not a blocker. However, the mutable Set[str] form allows _LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS.add() calls at runtime — which is an immutability gap relative to the frozenset form. Hardening addition: after Phase 2 additions, both files must use frozenset for _LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS. If aligning the type in narrative_report_compiler_v1.py requires changing 'Set[str]' to 'FrozenSet[str]' and the assignment form, this is a formatting-class change not a logic change; it is permitted under Phase 2 scope. Cursor must not change the members, only the container type. Record in Phase 1 verification.

narrative_report_compiler_v1.py:34 — _ENTITIES_PATH = 'knowledge_bus/interpretation_entities_v1/benchmark_interpretation_entities_v1.yaml'. :35 — _PATHWAY_PATH = 'knowledge_bus/pathway_explainers_v1/pathway_explainers_v1.yaml'. :36 — _FUNCTIONAL_PATH = 'knowledge_bus/functional_interpretation_v1/functional_interpretation_v1.yaml'. All three KB YAML files confirmed present (Glob). SOP Phase 3 content additions target the correct files.

knowledge_bus/interpretation_entities_v1/benchmark_interpretation_entities_v1.yaml:20-35 — existing entries confirm required fields: interpretation_entity_id, compiler_role, phenotype_id, idl_internal_id, pathway_explainer_id, functional_interpretation_domain_id. Existing lead entity uses compiler_role: benchmark_lead_domain (line 21); secondary uses benchmark_secondary_domain (line 31). New iron and thyroid entities must use compiler_role: benchmark_lead_domain. SOP Phase 3A field list matches schema exactly.

narrative_report_compiler_v1.py:623-626 — entity loop begins: rows = (entities_doc or {}).get('interpretation_entities'); if isinstance(rows, list): for row in rows: if not isinstance(row, dict): [loop continues]. The loop body past line 626 was not read during hardening. The compiler has one lead slot; if loop logic is last-match, multiple benchmark_lead_domain entries will collide silently (last one wins regardless of signal). SOP Phase 3A addresses this with a STOP gate but does not front-load the verification into Phase 1. Hardening addition: Phase 1 targeted reads must include narrative_report_compiler_v1.py lines 626-670 (entity loop body) to determine first-match vs last-match semantics BEFORE Phase 3A begins. Cursor must record the finding explicitly in Phase 1 output. If last-match: entity ordering in YAML is load-bearing and must be explicitly controlled for iron and thyroid entries. If first-match: only the first matching lead entity fires regardless of ordering. Either way, Cursor must state the semantics and confirm the collision behaviour is handled before proceeding to YAML authoring.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:72-83 — 'Important limitation' section explicitly defers: frame-level routing for signal_iron_low absolute vs functional distinction; signal_homocysteine_high B-vitamin vs renal distinction; simultaneous prose for all 6 domains; more than one lead block; routing redesign; entity-loop semantics changes; per-frame signal identity additions; ranking/scoring changes. Phase 3B pathway explainer content: 'covers iron-low and iron-high as a domain-level pattern only; does not distinguish absolute vs functional iron-low at routing level'. Acceptance criteria AC21/AC22 confirm carry-forward requirement.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:118-165 — Knowledge Bus content scope confined to: benchmark_interpretation_entities_v1.yaml (Phase 3A), pathway_explainers_v1.yaml (Phase 3B), functional_interpretation_v1.yaml (Phase 3C). No other KB files in content scope. PSI package files listed as 'read-only source reference' only (out-of-scope line 207-208). Scope advisory STOP-3 gate confirmed: any compiler infrastructure change escalates immediately.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:376-401 — Phase 4 test list includes: test_iron_low_signal_triggers_lead_pathway_block, test_iron_high_signal_triggers_lead_pathway_block, test_thyroid_ft3_low_triggers_lead_pathway_block, test_thyroid_tpoab_triggers_lead_pathway_block. Regression tests: test_homocysteine_lead_block_unchanged, test_lipid_secondary_block_unchanged, test both Python hint sets remain aligned. Narrative output tests: representative payload with each new signal as top finding produces non-placeholder lead narrative. Claim boundary tests. Missing-block fallback test. Coverage matches user hardening instruction.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:406-420 — Phase 5 carry-forward YAML must record: signal_iron_low absolute vs functional routing deferred; signal_homocysteine_high B-vitamin vs renal routing deferred; full six-domain simultaneous prose deferred (one-lead/one-secondary model constraint); routing redesign deferred as P2-FRAME-ROUTING-ARCHITECTURE-1; any entity-loop collision or ordering limitation discovered; any stopped content block with exact reason. Both deferrals required by user hardening instruction present.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:324-365 — content source authority limited to P1-25 thyroid PSI/sprint report/compiled card evidence and P1-26 iron PSI/sprint report/compiled card evidence. 'Do not invent medical content.' Prohibited iron wording listed (8 items including 'iron deficiency diagnosis', 'anaemia of inflammation', 'haemochromatosis', treatment advice). Prohibited thyroid wording listed (6 items including 'hypothyroidism diagnosis', 'Hashimoto's disease', 'your immune system is attacking your thyroid', treatment advice). Claim boundary assertions required in test suite.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:189-216 — 'Files out of scope' explicitly names: narrative_payload_builder_v1.py, narrative_brief_enforcement_v1.py, narrative_payload_v1.py, narrative_report_v1.py, orchestrator.py, results.py, scoring files, signal activation files, parser files, questionnaire files, frontend files, Gemini files, report redesign files, PSI package files, compiled card files (except read-only), WBC/lymphocyte/neutrophil files, TSAT calculated-mode files, TgAb/TPOAb euthyroid files. Acceptance criteria AC20 repeats the prohibition. User hardening instruction list fully covered.

advisory_inheritance_status.gpt_risk_classification: HIGH/MIXED (post-STOP-1, Intelligence Core Python changes required)
advisory_inheritance_status.escalation_rationale: STOP-1 revealed _LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS controls runtime pathway inclusion and requires Python changes in Intelligence Core files. Advisory OUT scope explicitly listed these files; after STOP-1 they moved IN. HIGH/MIXED replaces advisory STANDARD/CONTENT correctly. No material divergence on KB YAML scope, carry-forward requirements, or out-of-scope signals.
advisory_inheritance_status.material_departure_from_advisory: Two hardening additions beyond SOP draft scope: (1) _LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS type mismatch between files (frozenset vs Set[str]) — Cursor must align to frozenset after additions; (2) entity-loop semantics verification must be front-loaded into Phase 1 before Phase 3A YAML authoring begins — loop body at narrative_report_compiler_v1.py:626+ not read in SOP draft; first-match vs last-match semantics are load-bearing for multi-entity lead safety.

overall_verdict: HARDENED. All five front-matter fields confirmed valid. HIGH/MIXED escalation from advisory STANDARD/CONTENT is architecturally correct after STOP-1 revealed Intelligence Core Python changes required. SINGLE_PHASE confirmed — no assembler-layer gated allowlist switch. Behaviour scope confirmed to exactly two Python files with _LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS additions only. Both hint sets verified identical pre-sprint; iron and thyroid signals absent from both; homocysteine lead and lipid secondary members present. KB YAML file paths confirmed against compiler hardwired paths. Interpretation entity schema verified. Tests cover all four new signals plus homocysteine lead and lipid secondary regression. Carry-forward requirements for frame-level routing and six-domain prose confirmed. Two hardening additions: (1) align _LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS container type to frozenset in both files; (2) Phase 1 must read entity-loop body (narrative_report_compiler_v1.py:626-670) and record first-match vs last-match semantics before Phase 3A YAML authoring. Sprint cleared for kernel start.
